title: Relative Clauses
subtitle: Defining vs. non-defining — and why punctuation changes meaning
intro: Relative clauses let you pack two ideas into one sentence without repetition. A writer who cannot combine 'I have a friend. My friend speaks five languages.' into 'I have a friend who speaks five languages.' is stuck at Band 5. The Band 7+ writer distinguishes defining (which restricts meaning, no commas) from non-defining (which adds information, commas) — a comma here literally changes what the sentence means.

branches.label: Non-defining (commas)
children: 'My brother, who lives in Berlin, is a doctor.'

branches.label: Prep + which/whom
children: Formal: 'the policy to which we refer'
Avoid stranding prepositions in academic writing

rules.heading: Relative pronouns
rules.body: who (people), which (things), that (people or things, defining only), whose (possession), whom (formal object), where (place), when (time), why (reason).

rules.heading: Defining: no commas, no 'which' substitute for 'that'
rules.body: 'The students who attended the seminar passed the exam.' (Only those students.) Pronoun cannot be dropped if it is the subject.

rules.heading: Non-defining: commas, not 'that'
rules.body: 'The students, who had all attended the seminar, passed the exam.' (All students, and incidentally they all attended.) Cannot omit the pronoun.

rules.heading: Prepositions and reduced forms
rules.body: Formal: 'the topic about which she spoke'; informal: 'the topic that she spoke about' / 'the topic she spoke about'. Object relatives can drop 'that/which' in defining clauses.

examples.sentence: Policies that address root causes, rather than symptoms, tend to have lasting impact.
The Kyoto Protocol, which was signed in 1997, marked a turning point in climate diplomacy.
The country whose emissions grew the most between 2000 and 2010 was China.
I remember the moment when I first realised I wanted to teach.
Cities in which public transport is cheap and reliable see fewer cars on the road.

band6.text: Students that study daily, they get better grades than the students who don't.
band6.issue: Redundant resumptive pronoun 'they'; also reads as run-on.

band8.text: Students who study daily — particularly those whose routines include active recall — consistently outperform their less disciplined peers.
band8.why: Tight defining clause + inserted non-defining clause 'particularly those whose…' layers information without losing grammaticality.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers deploy non-defining clauses to add qualifying information without breaking flow: 'The policy, which has been widely criticised, is unlikely to survive the next election.' They also use participle-reduction: 'Students attending the seminar' instead of 'Students who attended the seminar' — more compact and academically fluent (this leads naturally into Module 3's participle clauses).

common_errors.error: 'The book what I read was excellent.'
common_errors.fix: 'What' is not a relative pronoun for objects → 'The book that/which I read was excellent.'

common_errors.error: Using 'that' in non-defining clauses: 'My sister, that lives in Paris…'
Doubling the object: 'The report that I read it was outdated.'
common_errors.fix: Drop the object pronoun → 'The report that I read was outdated.'

common_errors.error: Missing comma where needed: 'My father who is a doctor told me.'
common_errors.fix: If you have only one father → add commas: 'My father, who is a doctor, told me.'

practice.title: Combine with a relative clause
practice.instruction: Merge the two sentences using a relative clause. Decide whether it is defining or non-defining.

items.prompt: The museum reopened last month. It had been closed for renovation.
items.answer: The museum, which had been closed for renovation, reopened last month.
items.explanation: One museum, extra info → non-defining with commas.

items.prompt: Candidates have a second language. They are usually prioritised by employers.
items.answer: Candidates who have a second language are usually prioritised by employers.

items.prompt: Shanghai is a mega-city. Its population has tripled since 1990.
items.answer: Shanghai, whose population has tripled since 1990, is a mega-city.

items.prompt: There are some issues. The committee must address these issues today.
items.answer: There are some issues (that/which) the committee must address today.
items.explanation: Object relative pronoun can be omitted.

items.prompt: The report criticised the policy. It was published in The Guardian.
items.answer: The report, which was published in The Guardian, criticised the policy.

items.prompt: I still remember the day. I arrived in this country on that day.
items.answer: I still remember the day (when) I arrived in this country.

items.prompt: The main reason is cost. Public transport has not expanded for that reason.
items.answer: The main reason (why) public transport has not expanded is cost.

items.prompt: The professor is my supervisor. I collaborated with her on the paper.
items.answer: The professor with whom I collaborated on the paper is my supervisor. / The professor (who/that) I collaborated with on the paper is my supervisor.

practice.title: Choose the relative pronoun
practice.instruction: Fill in with who / which / that / whose / where / when.

items.prompt: The factory ___ was built in 1920 is now a museum.
Einstein, ___ theories revolutionised physics, was a patent clerk.
This is the village ___ I grew up.
Employees ___ skills become outdated are often reassigned.
The 1960s was a decade ___ civil rights movements gathered pace.
Candidates ___ submit late applications will be disqualified.
Solar energy, ___ costs have fallen dramatically, now rivals fossil fuels.
That is the reason ___ the project was cancelled.

practice.title: Defining or non-defining?
practice.instruction: Pick the option with correct punctuation and pronoun.

items.prompt: My sister ___ a surgeon in Singapore.
items.explanation: Assuming one sister, non-defining with commas.

items.prompt: Students ___ the lecture reported higher test scores.
The documentary ___ on Sunday won three awards.
Paris ___ I spent my twenties, remains my favourite city.
The applicant ___ I interviewed last week was the strongest candidate.
There are several factors ___ influence academic performance.
items.explanation: 'That' or 'which' for things; subject relative cannot be dropped.

items.prompt: The method ___ she described is widely used today.
items.explanation: 'What' is never a relative pronoun in standard English.

items.prompt: The city, ___ a population of 12 million, is the economic hub of the region.
